title: Platnum Thinpack collection and EOE question
display_title: Platnum Thinpack collection and EOE question
wikitext:
[]: Platnum Thinpack collection and EOE question?             Quote Post by Gretnablue. » Fri Nov 22, 2013 11:21 pm For a year I've been looking for a copy of the Evangelion series, however the prices are absolutely ridiculous like one asking for over £1000 three months age (can't find it to send a link). However yesterday I bought a version of the platinum thin pack (NTSC) for £80. Now one things that seems strange is that the set comes with 6 volumes while I know there is 7 platinum volumes. Can someone explain this? Also what is the best version of End of Evangelion in the UK that doesn't have the gamma raised in the first two minutes?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 22, 2013 11:51 pm The other three versions of Platinum are on 7 discs and include some bonus material. Trust me when I say that most of that bonus material is frivolous and you're not missing much. The Platinum Thinpack does not have bonus features, but it does have higher encoding rates than the other versions, so what you're getting will sound and look a little bit better, which I think personally is preferable.
